perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington, without regard to choice of law principles that may otherwise compel the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction. Each of the parties hereby irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts sitting in the State of Washington to resolve any dispute arising from this Agreement and waives any defense of inconvenient or improper forum.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington, without regard to choice of law principles that may otherwise compel the application of the laws of any other jurisdiction. The terms and provisions of this agreement shall be deemed severable, and in the event any term or provision hereof or portion thereof is deemed or held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable, such provision shall be conformed to prevailing law rather than voided, if possible, in order to achieve the intent of the parties, and, in any event, the remaining terms and provisions of this agreement shall nevertheless continue and be deemed to be in full force and effect and binding upon the parties. Each of the parties hereby irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts sitting in the State of Washington to resolve any dispute arising from this Agreement and waives any defense of inconvenient or improper forum.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the jurisdiction and venue clause to the end of the severability clause suggests it only applies if the jurisdiction clause itself is valid and enforceable. This potentially weakens the enforceability of the jurisdiction clause if a portion of the contract is deemed invalid.
perturbation.law_explanation: 28 U.S.C. § 1391 governs venue in federal courts. While parties can agree to a forum selection clause, moving the clause to the end of the severability clause implies that the agreed-upon venue is only valid if all other clauses are enforceable, which contradicts the intent of having a specifically designated venue. A court may have to throw the clause out, and find venue elsewhere.

Venue generally (a) Applicability of Section .-Except as otherwise provided by law- (1) this section shall govern the venue of all civil actions brought in district courts of the United States; and (2) the proper venue for a civil action shall be determined without regard to whether the action is local or transitory in nature. perturbation.justification: The altered contract introduces a structural flaw by positioning the jurisdiction clause within the scope of the severability clause. This implies that the enforceability of the jurisdiction clause depends on the validity of the other clauses. 28 U.S.C. § 1391, as indicated in 'Scraped Law Snippet 1,' generally governs venue in federal courts. By making the jurisdiction clause contingent, the changed text weakens the parties' agreement on a specific venue, creating a potential conflict with the intent of having a designated venue and undermining the certainty that 28 U.S.C. § 1391 seeks to provide.
